steps.title: Spot the antecedent
steps.content: The blank is the object of “making” and must refer to Marché Monge, the market. Marché Monge is singular.

steps.title: Match the pronoun
steps.content:
Identify the grammar issue: pronoun-antecedent agreement. The antecedent is "Marché Monge" — a single market, singular. Rule: a pronoun must match its antecedent in number and must refer clearly to a specific noun.
  • Choice A — those: Plural demonstrative pronoun — does not match the singular antecedent Marché Monge. ✗
  • Choice B — it: Singular pronoun. Correctly refers to the single market Marché Monge. ✓
  • Choice C — these: Plural demonstrative — same number error as A. ✗
  • Choice D — one: Indefinite pronoun that refers to an unspecified member of a group, not a specific named entity. Creates vagueness. ✗
That confirms B is correct.
